description: Literature coverage/contact-area database for k_spread calibration of plastic_coverage.py against DEM outputs. Each entry captures exact process conditions needed to decide comparability with our single-compression pellet setup.

our_baseline.metric_type: 3D surface area coverage (DEM contact-area / AM surface)
our_baseline.note: DEM value is BEFORE k_spread calibration to physical coverage

entries.citation: Lee et al., Nature Communications 15, 2024 — Shear force effect of the dry process on cathode contact coverage in all-solid-state batteries

coverage_metric.definition: coverage = (SE-touching AM perimeter) / (total AM perimeter); yellow=AM edge, red=void edge, remainder=SE contact

si_highlights.why_useful: Direct comparison base for our contact-model constants
Confirms coverage = perimeter ratio (2D), not 3D surface area; lower bound vs our 3D DEM value
si_highlights.impact_on_our_k_spread: Cannot directly equate 30.6% (2D perimeter) to our 22% (3D surface). Need perimeter-to-surface geometric factor (~1.3-1.5x for spherical AM).

assembly_order: cathode pressed at 7 TON on SE

si_highlights.why_useful: Performance vs coverage trend establishes coverage as dominant variable

extracted.note: Dry > Wet > Hand for rate capability, correlating with coverage

si_highlights.why_useful: Their DEM also uses Hooke/Hysteresis or Hertz — can reuse as benchmark

comparability_with_ours.pressure_match: PARTIAL (their 520 MPa vs our 300 MPa — ~73% pressure, expect slightly lower coverage)
comparability_with_ours.chemistry_match: CLOSE (NCM532 vs our NCM, LPSCl vs our LPSCl)

k_spread_implication.caveat: 2D-to-3D correction needed first; true target likely 0.35-0.45 in 3D terms → k_spread ≈ 1.26-1.43

entries.citation: Bielefeld, Weber, Janek. J. Phys. Chem. C 2019, 123, 1626-1634 — Microstructural Modeling of Composite Cathodes for All-Solid-State Batteries
entries.caveat_upfront: Does NOT report coverage (%) directly. Reports specific active interface area A_spec,a (m²/m³) and utilization level. Also does NOT give τ(composition) or σ_eff(composition) curves despite earlier expectation. Still Tier 1 because it validates percolation exponent and optimal composition for our scaling law.

process.pressure_match_with_ours: NO (no physical pressure, just geometric packing)
process.overlap_treatment: AM spheres = NO overlap (hard-sphere packing, <1e-5 vol% residual); SE polyhedra = WITH overlap; merge step assigns overlap to AM (SE loses volume), AM stays spherical

key_results.beta_comparison: Matches Sur et al. 1976 3D site-percolation simple-cubic lattice — VALIDATES our scaling law using percolation-like exponents (CN^1.5, √(φ-0.2), etc.)

electrode_thickness_effect.effect_on_thin_electrode_percolation: thin electrodes appear to percolate at lower AM vol% — FINITE SIZE ARTIFACT, not real advantage

coverage_equivalent_derivation.how_to_convert_to_coverage_frac: coverage ≈ A_spec,a / A_spec,geo, where A_spec,geo = g_AM^V · 6/d_AM (eq 7)
coverage_equivalent_derivation.confidence: LOW — requires reading plot values; authors did not tabulate

comparability_with_ours.chemistry_match: CLOSE (NCM + LPS thiophosphate, like our NMC+LPSCl)
comparability_with_ours.metric_match: PARTIAL (3D volumetric — can be converted to 3D surface fraction with geometric factor)
usefulness_for_us: VALIDATES our scaling law percolation exponent ~sqrt(phi-0.2) and CN^1.5 forms
PROVIDES optimal composition anchors (80/20 wt at 5% porosity) consistent with our dataset peak
OFFERS particle-size / percolation-threshold relationship that can test CN(d) dependence
DOES NOT directly calibrate k_spread — no headline coverage value

open_questions: How to map their (AM-no-overlap + SE-polyhedra-with-overlap) to our (hard-sphere + plastic delta overlap) geometry? Their overlap-reassignment to AM already mimics plastic-like contact area expansion.
Their β=0.41 comes from electronic cluster percolation; does ionic cluster (SE-mediated) obey same exponent?
Can we regenerate their A_spec,a vs composition curves with our DEM + network solver as validation?
